page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system features dedicated CPU allocation, a 38 MB L3 cache, and supports AVX-512 vector extensions. It is equipped with 340.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.08 GB per core. Acceleration is driven by four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a combined 160 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between CPU and GPU capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 25% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the middle 50% average. Conversely, the server achieves top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference, specifically in prompt processing across 135M, 7B, and 70B models, as well as text generation for 7B and 70B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of GPU resources and VRAM relative to the CPU allocation makes this server highly specialized. It represents a significant hardware investment where value is realized almost exclusively through GPU-bound workloads rather than general-purpose computing. The lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning, which should be factored into the overall architecture. This server is suited for large language model inference, deep learning training, and parallel scientific computing workloads that can fully leverage the four NVIDIA A100 GPUs.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for GPU-intensive workloads. It features 48 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz, paired with 340.0 GB of system memory. The primary hardware highlight is the inclusion of four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs delivering a total of 160 GB of VRAM, while local storage is absent. Performance benchmarks indicate weak single-core CPU execution, placing in the bottom 25%, and average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. However, the server excels in LLM inference, achieving top-tier results in prompt processing and text generation for medium and large models. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for specialized parallel processing tasks but inefficient for standard CPU-bound applications. It is best suited for deep learning, large-scale AI inference, and GPU-accelerated data science.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g features 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 340GB RAM, and 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs with 160GB VRAM. It delivers top-tier LLM inference performance but has weak single-core CPU speeds.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 340GB RAM, and 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs for AI and LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform combining four NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 340GB RAM for top-tier parallel computing and language model inference.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 160 GB total VRAM.
48 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon x86_64 processors at 2.2 GHz.
340.0 GB of system memory with dedicated CPU allocation.
Top-tier benchmark performance in LLM prompt processing and text generation.
No local storage included, requiring external storage attachment.
Best suited for deep learning, AI inference, and parallel scientific computing.
